title: Phanera havilandii
intro: Phanera havilandii, commonly known as Indian coral tree or Indian coral, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Phanera havilandii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phanera havilandii is often used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Phanera havilandii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Phanera and is native to Southeast Asia. The exact classification may be subject to revision as new research emerges. The plant is often associated with tropical forests and is typically found growing epiphytically. Further studies are needed to confirm the accuracy of its classification.
